title: Advanced Priiloader Usage
warnings: !!Never set autoboot to "BootMii IOS"!! — this creates a boot loop where you have to hold RESET every single time just to reach the Priiloader menu.
_note: This is a reference guide for features Priiloader already has once it's installed — nothing here needs an SD card or USB drive, so there's no storage picker for this one.

steps.title: What Priiloader can do beyond brick protection
steps.body:
Once installed, Priiloader is more than just brick protection — it can **autoboot straight into a homebrew app**, the Homebrew Channel, or its own menu, and it has a long list of **System Menu Hacks** for things like blocking updates, region-freeing, fixing WiiConnect24's clock issue, or patching games for Wiimmfi.

The full, up-to-date hacks list and FAQ live on the official Priiloader documentation — this guide covers the two things people set up most: autoboot and finding your way around the hacks list.

Enter Priiloader any time by holding **RESET** while powering on (or while already on the Wii Menu, on Wii Family Edition).

steps.title: Autoboot into a homebrew app
steps.body:
To have your Wii boot straight into a specific homebrew app (like USB Loader GX) instead of the System Menu:

1. In Priiloader, use the **file install** option to install the app's `.dol`/`.elf` file as the one Priiloader launches.
2. Go to **Settings**.
3. Find the autoboot option and cycle it to **Installed file**.
4. Save settings, then return to the System Menu.

From then on, powering on the Wii normally launches straight into that app. Hold **RESET** at power-on to get back into Priiloader if you need to change it.

**Using USB Loader GX specifically?** Install its forwarder WAD and channel booter first — the guide for that loader covers this — then point autoboot at the forwarder instead of a raw file.

steps.title: Autoboot into the Homebrew Channel or Priiloader itself
steps.body:
If you'd rather boot straight into the **Homebrew Channel**, or straight into **Priiloader's own menu** every time:

1. Go to **Settings** in Priiloader.
2. Cycle the autoboot option to **Homebrew Channel** or **Priiloader Menu**.
3. Save settings.

!!Do not set this to "BootMii IOS"!! — that option boots straight into BootMii's low-level menu, and you'd need to hold RESET at every power-on just to reach Priiloader again.

steps.title: Finding your way around System Menu Hacks
steps.body:
The **System Menu Hacks** menu is where most of Priiloader's useful features live — things like:

- **Block Disc Updates** / **Block Online Updates** — stop Nintendo or game discs from forcing a system update
- **Region Free** patches — play discs from other regions
- **Wiimmfi patch** — auto-patch games for online play (see the `nintendowfc` guide)
- **WiiConnect24 clock fixes** — needed on Wii U's vWii for some WiiLink channels (see the `wiiconnect24` guide)
- Display fixes (480p, deflicker) for CRT vs. non-CRT setups

Scroll through the list, press **A** to toggle a hack on, then scroll to **save settings** and press **A** again — nothing takes effect until you save.

If a hack you expect to see is missing, your `hacks_hash.ini` file (Priiloader's list of known hacks) may be outdated — reinstalling Priiloader or grabbing an updated hacks file fixes this.

steps.title: That's the overview
steps.body: Priiloader's menu is worth exploring beyond these — poke around **Settings** and **System Menu Hacks** to see everything available for your console. Just remember: nothing sticks until you save, and avoid the BootMii IOS autoboot trap.
